title: Strawberry Hallucination Detection — Failure Mode
description: MedGemma extracts 'rash: yes' with evidence_quote 'no rash observed'. The quote IS in the note (passes evidence grounding), but Strawberry detects the quote CONTRADICTS the claim.
note_text: Child 5yo M fever 2 days cough runny nose no rash observed no diarrhea drinking well referred health center

evidence_grounding_result.note: All evidence_quotes are substrings of the note — grounding check passes

flagged_claims.reason: Evidence 'no rash observed' contradicts 'rash: yes' — the quote negates the claim

lesson: Evidence grounding (substring check) catches MISSING evidence. Strawberry catches CONTRADICTORY evidence — a more subtle hallucination mode where the model cites a quote that actually says the opposite of the claim.
